---
title: Cursor
description: Cursor adapter outputs, config toggles, and sync behavior.
section: adapters
---

## Cursor

The Cursor adapter converts Syntra's `ai/` source files into Cursor Rules files.

## Generated files

When Cursor sync runs, Syntra writes:

- `.cursor/rules/*.mdc`

Each generated rule file is derived from your instruction and agent definitions.

## Configuration

Cursor behavior is controlled in `ai/config.yml`:

```yaml
adapters:
	cursor:
		enabled: false
		instructionsMode: individual
```

| Key | Effect |
|---|---|
| `enabled` | If `true`, Cursor output is included in default `syntra sync` |
| `instructionsMode` | Controls how instruction content is split before rendering (`concatenated` or `individual`) |

This project currently sets `adapters.cursor.enabled: false`, so standard sync does not update `.cursor/`.

## Sync behavior

Use:

```bash
syntra sync --cursor
```

or enable Cursor and run a full sync:

```bash
syntra sync
```

<Callout variant="warning" title="Do not maintain .cursor files by hand">
	Files in `.cursor/rules/` are generated output and are overwritten by `syntra sync`.
</Callout>
